No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on 'monetary and fiscal policy' and 'global macroeconomic issues' provides foundational knowledge for creating sound policy frameworks to address poverty (Target 1.b).
evidence: interactions between monetary and fiscal policy

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to health, well-being, or medical issues.

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3), develops skills for real-world issues and employment (Target 4.4), and explicitly addresses 'sustainability issues' (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly addresses 'international macroeconomics' (Target 8.1), 'Production Economy' (Target 8.2), 'Unemployment' (Target 8.5), and 'International Capital Market Integration' (Target 8.10), all central to decent work and economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on 'International Capital Market Integration' and 'Capital Controls' provides a framework for understanding how financial resources can support infrastructure and industrial development (Targets 9.1, 9.3, 9.a).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course directly addresses 'global imbalances,' 'monetary and fiscal policy' (Target 10.4), and the regulation of global financial markets (Target 10.5), all crucial for reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the course's macroeconomic focus does not explicitly or implicitly cover responsible consumption and production patterns, waste, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: The course's topic 'processes of adjustment to shocks' can be weakly inferred to include economic shocks caused by climate-related hazards, aligning with strengthening resilience (Target 13.1).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to marine ecosystems or life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's topic 'Capital Controls' is relevant to managing financial flows and potentially reducing illicit flows (Target 16.4). The study of 'international macroeconomics' also contributes to understanding global economic institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.8).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's core focus on 'international macroeconomics,' 'global imbalances,' and 'monetary and fiscal policy interactions' directly addresses enhancing global macroeconomic stability and policy coherence (Targets 17.13, 17.14).
